scheme_name: Visit of Contract Farming Farmers to the Research Stations in Karnataka
page_title: Visit of Contract Farming Farmers to the Research Stations in Karnataka

details: The scheme “Visit of Contract Farming Farmers to the Research Stations in Karnataka” by the Agriculture-Farmers Welfare Department, Tamil Nadu, aims to enhance the agricultural practices of contract farming farmers by enabling visits to research stations in Karnataka. A batch of 50 farmers will be sponsored for a 3-day visit. The scheme focuses on increasing exposure to advanced techniques and research findings to improve farming outcomes.
benefits: ₹1,12,500/- per batch of 50 farmers for 3 days.
Exposure to the latest agricultural research and innovations.
eligibility: The beneficiary should be a Farmer.
The beneficiary should be a resident of Tamil Nadu.

offline: Collect the application form from the nearest Agricultural Office.
Fill out the application form with the required details and attach mandatory documents (self-attested, if required).
Submit the form to the concerned office:
Submit the application to the Assistant Agricultural Officer.
Submit to the Agricultural Officer or Deputy Agricultural Officer.
Submit to the Joint Director of Agriculture.

documents_required: Proof of identity (Aadhar card, Voter ID).
Land ownership documents or lease agreement.

faqs.question: What is the purpose of the scheme?
faqs.answer: The scheme aims to help farmers to learn about new agricultural techniques through research station visits.

faqs.question: Who is eligible for the scheme?
faqs.answer: All farmers of the Tamil Nadu State, with special provisions for SC/ST and women farmers eligible for the scheme.

faqs.question: How long is the research station visit?
Is there any financial support provided under this scheme?
faqs.answer: Yes, ₹1,12,500/- per batch of 50 farmers shall be provided as financial support.

faqs.question: Are SC/ST farmers given priority?
faqs.answer: Yes, 24% of the farmers selected will be from SC/ST communities.

faqs.question: Is the scheme available for women farmers?
faqs.answer: Yes, 20% of the beneficiaries will be women farmers or women groups.

faqs.question: How is the funding utilized?
faqs.answer: Funding is used to cover travel, lodging, and other expenses during the visit.

faqs.question: What documents are required to apply?
faqs.answer: Farmer ID, proof of identity, land ownership or contract farming proof, caste certificate (for SC/ST farmers), and documents for women groups.

faqs.question: Is there any age limit for the scheme?
faqs.answer: No, there is no specific age limit mentioned.

faqs.question: How many farmers can participate in one batch?
faqs.answer: A batch consisting of 50 farmers can participate.
